questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Aasana' in Yoga and its importance in a person's life. (a) Define Aasana, (b) Mention its benefits, and (c) List two Aasanas and their effects on the body. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Aasana is a fundamental concept in Yoga that refers to the physical postures or positions assumed by a person during practice. It is essential for overall well-being, balance, and harmony in life. (a) Aasana is a Sanskrit word that means 'posture' or 'pose.' It involves holding a specific position for a certain period, focusing on the breath, and aligning the body correctly. (b) The benefits of Aasana include improved flexibility, balance, strength, and posture. It also helps in reducing stress, anxiety, and fatigue. (c) Two Aasanas and their effects on the body are: i) 'Tadasana' (Mountain Pose): It helps in building good posture, balance, and strengthens the spine. ii) 'Viparita Karani' (Legs Up The Wall Pose): It reduces swelling in the legs, relieves stress, and promotes relaxation.
marking_scheme: Define Aasana and its importance (1 mark)
Mention two benefits of Aasana (1 mark)
List two Aasanas and their effects on the body (3 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the concept of 'Pranayama' in Yoga and its significance in a person's life. (a) Define Pranayama, (b) Explain its benefits, and (c) List two Pranayamas and their effects on the body. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Pranayama is a vital component of Yoga that involves the control of breath, or 'Prana,' to balance the body and mind. It plays a significant role in a person's life by promoting physical, mental, and emotional well-being. (a) Pranayama is a Sanskrit word that means 'life force' or 'breath control.' It involves regulating the breath, focusing on the mind, and aligning the body correctly. (b) The benefits of Pranayama include improved lung capacity, reduced stress, and increased oxygenation of the body. It also helps in balancing the nervous system and promoting relaxation. (c) Two Pranayamas and their effects on the body are: i) 'Bhastrika Pranayama' (Bellows Breath): It increases oxygenation, reduces stress, and strengthens the lungs. ii) 'Anuloma Viloma' (Alternate Nostril Breathing): It balances the nervous system, promotes relaxation, and reduces anxiety.
marking_scheme: Define Pranayama and its importance (1 mark)
Mention two benefits of Pranayama (1 mark)
List two Pranayamas and their effects on the body (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Drishti' in Yoga and its importance in maintaining focus and concentration. (a) Define Drishti, (b) Mention its benefits, and (c) List three Drishtis and their effects on the body. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Drishti is a fundamental concept in Yoga that refers to the focal point or the point of concentration during a posture. It plays a significant role in maintaining focus and concentration. (a) Drishti is a Sanskrit word that means 'to see' or 'focal point.' It involves focusing on a specific point, aligning the body correctly, and regulating the breath. (b) The benefits of Drishti include improved concentration, balance, and coordination. It also helps in reducing distractions, promoting relaxation, and increasing mental clarity. (c) Three Drishtis and their effects on the body are: i) 'Nasikagra Drishti' (Tip of the Nose): It helps in focusing, reducing distractions, and promoting relaxation. ii) 'Bhrumadhya Drishti' (Third Eye Point): It improves concentration, promotes mental clarity, and reduces stress. iii) 'Angusthamadhye Drishti' (Tip of the Thumb): It enhances focus, balance, and coordination.
marking_scheme: Define Drishti and its importance (1 mark)
Mention two benefits of Drishti (1 mark)
List three Drishtis and their effects on the body (3 marks)

questions.q: Describe the concept of 'Bandha' in Yoga and its significance in a person's life. (a) Define Bandha, (b) Explain its benefits, and (c) List three Bandhas and their effects on the body. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Bandha is a fundamental concept in Yoga that refers to the energy locks or the contraction of specific muscles during a posture. It plays a significant role in a person's life by promoting physical, mental, and emotional well-being. (a) Bandha is a Sanskrit word that means 'lock' or 'contraction.' It involves contracting specific muscles, regulating the breath, and aligning the body correctly. (b) The benefits of Bandha include improved energy, reduced stress, and increased vitality. It also helps in balancing the nervous system, promoting relaxation, and enhancing focus. (c) Three Bandhas and their effects on the body are: i) 'Jalandhara Bandha' (Throat Lock): It reduces stress, promotes relaxation, and enhances focus. ii) 'Uddiyana Bandha' (Abdominal Lock): It improves digestion, reduces bloating, and increases vitality. iii) 'Mula Bandha' (Root Lock): It enhances energy, improves circulation, and promotes relaxation.
marking_scheme: Define Bandha and its importance (1 mark)
Mention two benefits of Bandha (1 mark)
List three Bandhas and their effects on the body (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Yama' and 'Niyama' in Yoga and their importance in a person's life. (a) Define Yama and Niyama, (b) Mention their benefits, and (c) List two Yamas and two Niyamas and their effects on the body. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Yama and Niyama are fundamental concepts in Yoga that refer to the observances and practices that promote physical, mental, and emotional well-being. They play a significant role in a person's life by cultivating a positive mindset, behavior, and lifestyle. (a) Yama refers to the five abstentions or the five non-violent practices, while Niyama refers to the five observances or the five positive practices. (b) The benefits of Yama and Niyama include improved relationships, reduced stress, and increased self-awareness. They also help in promoting physical and mental well-being, and enhancing spiritual growth. (c) Two Yamas and two Niyamas and their effects on the body are: i) Yama: (a) Ahimsa (Non-violence): Promotes compassion, empathy, and kindness. (b) Satya (Truthfulness): Enhances honesty, integrity, and self-awareness. ii) Niyama: (a) Sauca (Purity): Cultivates cleanliness, hygiene, and mental clarity. (b) Svadhyaya (Self-study): Promotes self-awareness, introspection, and spiritual growth.
marking_scheme: Define Yama and Niyama and their importance (1 mark)
Mention two benefits of Yama and Niyama (1 mark)
List two Yamas and two Niyamas and their effects on the body (3 marks)
